canonical_definition: MMBench-Video is described in the provided literature as a benchmark for evaluating multimodal understanding. The single available definition characterizes it as a "video-focused version of the MMBench benchmark." The primary and sole application mentioned across the sources is its use to measure "general video understanding." Papers report using MMBench-Video to assess model performance and establish new state-of-the-art results for this capability. The provided data does not contain information about other constructs or behaviors measured by this instrument.
papers: Oryx MLLM: On-Demand Spatial-Temporal Understanding at Arbitrary Resolution
SVBench: A Benchmark with Temporal Multi-Turn Dialogues for Streaming Video Understanding
Unhackable Temporal Reward for Scalable Video MLLMs

relationships.evidence: In addition, we rigorously assess the performance of our Video-UTR on the recently introduced and exceptionally demanding long-range video understanding benchmark, MVBench-Video. (Section 4.4)
